title: Supabase Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Supabase** is an open-source Firebase alternative — a managed Postgres database fronted by PostgREST, GoTrue (Auth), Storage, Realtime, and Edge Functions, exposed at `https://{project_ref}.supabase.co`. This connector — `SupabaseConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "supabase"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps every operational surface a Shielva tenant typically needs:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| PostgREST | `/rest/v1/{table}` | CRUD with PostgREST filter syntax |
| Auth Admin (GoTrue) | `/auth/v1/admin/users` | List, get, create, update, delete users |
| Storage | `/storage/v1/{bucket,object}` | Buckets + objects (upload/download/delete/sign) |
| Edge Functions | `/functions/v1/{name}` | Invoke Deno-runtime serverless functions |
| RPC | `/rest/v1/rpc/{fn}` | Call SQL stored procedures |

The connector normalises rows + users + storage objects into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces every user-named operation as a standalone `async def` method (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::SupabaseHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Locate your Project URL and Service Role Key

1. Sign in to https://supabase.com and open your project.
2. Open **Project Settings → API**.
3. Copy the **Project URL** (e.g. `https://abcdwxyz.supabase.co`).
4. Under **Project API keys**, copy the **service_role** key (NOT the `anon` key). The service-role key bypasses Row-Level Security — treat it as a master secret.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Supabase**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `project_url` (text) | Full project URL — e.g. `https://abcdwxyz.supabase.co` |
| `service_role_key` (secret) | The `eyJ…` service-role JWT |
| `schema` (text, optional) | Postgres schema (default `public`) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number, optional) | Soft cap, default `100` |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `project_url` + `service_role_key` are present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `SupabaseHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which hits `GET /auth/v1/settings` as a lightweight, RLS-free probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → retried with backoff.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Supabase server-to-server uses **service-role API key authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Credential model

- `service_role_key` is sent in BOTH the `Authorization: Bearer <key>` header and the `apikey: <key>` header on every request. GoTrue requires the `apikey`; PostgREST requires the `Authorization` Bearer.
- `project_url` is the full Supabase project URL (`https://{ref}.supabase.co`). The connector also accepts a legacy `project_ref` short form and constructs the URL itself.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `project_url` + `service_role_key`. Initialises `SupabaseHTTPClient`. Does **not** call the API. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the configured key — no token exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /auth/v1/settings` — RLS-free probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None — the key lives in `self.config`. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization:   Bearer <service_role_key>
apikey:          <service_role_key>
Content-Type:    application/json        (when body present)
Accept-Profile:  <schema>                (PostgREST)
Content-Profile: <schema>                (PostgREST writes)
```

### Security

The service-role key has the same authority as the `postgres` role. NEVER expose it to a browser, NEVER log it. The platform stores it via `BaseConnector.save_config()` → sealed config / vault sidecar (port 8054). Audit middleware redactors include `service_role_key`.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `project_url` | string | yes | `self.project_url` | Full project URL |
| `service_role_key` | secret | yes | `self.service_role_key` | Sent as `apikey` + `Authorization: Bearer` |
| `schema` | string | no (default `public`) | `self.schema` | PostgREST `Accept-Profile` / `Content-Profile` |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no (default 100) | `self.rate_limit_per_min` | Client-side soft cap |

Legacy compatibility: `project_ref` short form (e.g. `abcdwxyz`) is also accepted and is expanded to `https://{ref}.supabase.co` automatically.

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["project_url", "service_role_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `SupabaseConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::SupabaseHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `project_url` + `service_role_key`; initialise `SupabaseHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a TokenInfo whose `access_token` is the configured key.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Probes `GET /auth/v1/settings`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
No-op by default. Supabase is not a single-stream resource — the orchestrator picks which tables/buckets/users to ingest via `list_rows()` / `list_users()` / `list_objects()` plus the normalizer helpers.

children.title: PostgREST (REST)
children.content:
### `async list_rows(table, columns="*", filter=None, order=None, limit=None, offset=None) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /rest/v1/{table}` — see Filter Syntax below. Alias: `select(...)`.

### `async get_row(table, row_id, columns="*", id_column="id") -> Optional[Dict]`
Shorthand for `list_rows(filter={id_column: row_id}, limit=1)` returning the first row or `None`.

### `async insert_row(table, rows, returning="representation") -> List[Dict]`
`POST /rest/v1/{table}` with `Prefer: return=<representation|minimal>`. Alias: `insert(...)`.

### `async update_row(table, filter, fields) -> List[Dict]`
`PATCH /rest/v1/{table}?<filter>`. Alias: `update(...)`.

### `async delete_row(table, filter) -> List[Dict]`
`DELETE /rest/v1/{table}?<filter>`. Alias: `delete(...)`.

### `async upsert(table, rows, on_conflict=None) -> List[Dict]`
`POST /rest/v1/{table}` with `Prefer: resolution=merge-duplicates,return=representation`. `on_conflict` lands as a query param.

### `async rpc(function_name, params=None) -> Any`
`POST /rest/v1/rpc/{function_name}` — invoke a Postgres stored function.

children.title: Auth Admin (GoTrue)
children.content:
### `async list_users(page=1, per_page=50) -> Dict`
`GET /auth/v1/admin/users?page=&per_page=`.

### `async get_user(user_id) -> Dict`
`GET /auth/v1/admin/users/{id}`.

### `async create_user(email, password=None, user_metadata=None, email_confirm=None) -> Dict`
`POST /auth/v1/admin/users`.

### `async update_user(user_id, attrs) -> Dict`
`PUT /auth/v1/admin/users/{id}` — partial update of `email`, `password`, `user_metadata`, `app_metadata`, `email_confirm`, `phone`.

### `async delete_user(user_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /auth/v1/admin/users/{id}`.

children.title: Storage
children.content:
### `async list_buckets() -> List[Dict]`
`GET /storage/v1/bucket` — list all buckets in the project.

### `async list_objects(bucket, prefix="", limit=100, offset=0) -> List[Dict]`
`POST /storage/v1/object/list/{bucket}` — list objects with optional prefix.

### `async upload_object(bucket, path, content, content_type="application/octet-stream", upsert=False, cache_control=None) -> Dict`
`POST /storage/v1/object/{bucket}/{path}`. Pass `upsert=True` to overwrite (`x-upsert: true` header).

### `async download_object(bucket, path) -> bytes`
`GET /storage/v1/object/{bucket}/{path}` — raw bytes.

### `async delete_object(bucket, path) -> Dict`
`DELETE /storage/v1/object/{bucket}/{path}`.

### `async create_signed_url(bucket, path, expires_in=3600) -> Dict`
`POST /storage/v1/object/sign/{bucket}/{path}` — short-lived signed URL for browser download.

children.title: Edge Functions
children.content:
### `async invoke_function(name, payload=None) -> Any`
`POST /functions/v1/{name}` — invoke a Deno-runtime Edge Function. Returns parsed JSON when present, else raw text.

sections.title: PostgREST Filter Syntax
sections.content:
All `filter` dicts on `list_rows` / `update_row` / `delete_row` are translated to PostgREST query syntax by `helpers/utils.py::_format_filter_value`:

| Python | URL |
|---|---|
| `{"id": 5}` | `id=eq.5` |
| `{"status": "active"}` | `status=eq.active` |
| `{"published": True}` | `published=eq.true` (lowercase!) |
| `{"rating": {"gt": 4}}` | `rating=gt.4` |
| `{"id": [1, 2, 3]}` | `id=in.(1,2,3)` |
| `{"name": {"like": "%foo%"}}` | `name=like.%foo%` |
| `{"title": {"ilike": "%foo%"}}` | `title=ilike.%foo%` |
| `{"deleted_at": {"is": "null"}}` | `deleted_at=is.null` |
| `{"tags": {"cs": "{a,b}"}}` | `tags=cs.{a,b}` (contains) |

Supported single-key operators: `eq`, `neq`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `like`, `ilike`, `is`, `in`, `cs`, `cd`, `sl`, `sr`, `nxr`, `nxl`, `adj`, `ov`, `fts`, `plfts`, `phfts`, `wfts`, `not`. Unknown operators are passed through verbatim so callers can adopt new PostgREST operators without bumping the connector.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
SupabaseError                       # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── SupabaseAuthError                # 401 / 403
├── SupabaseBadRequestError          # 400 / 422
├── SupabaseNotFoundError            # 404
├── SupabaseConflictError            # 409 (duplicate key)
├── SupabaseRateLimitError           # 429 — retry_after_s
└── SupabaseServerError              # 5xx + transport errors
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
SupabaseNotFound     = SupabaseNotFoundError
SupabaseNetworkError = SupabaseServerError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request` + `helpers/utils.py::with_retry`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 / 422 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff (0.5s, 1s, 2s), retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3`; honours `Retry-After` header |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `SupabaseServerError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
5xx → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   FAILED)
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `project_url` or `service_role_key` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from GoTrue — using anon key or the JWT is wrong project | Confirm you copied the **service_role** key, not the `anon` key; verify the project URL matches. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 401 from GoTrue — key expired or rotated | Regenerate the service-role key in Supabase Dashboard → Project Settings → API. |
| 4xx with `"new row violates row-level security policy"` | Caller used `anon` key on a RLS-protected table | Make sure the connector is configured with the service_role key — it bypasses RLS. |
| `supabase.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (rate cap) | The client retries with backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on `/storage/v1/object/{bucket}/{path}` | Bucket doesn't exist or path typo | Verify via `list_buckets()` / `list_objects(bucket, prefix=...)`. |
| 400 from PostgREST on a write | Body shape mismatch (e.g. extra column, missing NOT NULL) | Check the column definitions; PostgREST surfaces the Postgres error in `response_body["hint"]`. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'SupabaseNotFound'` | Older callers using the legacy alias | Already preserved — `SupabaseNotFound = SupabaseNotFoundError`. |
| Edge Function returns 401 | Function expects user JWT, not service-role | Functions can opt to read user JWT — pass the user's token via `Authorization` instead, or change the function's auth model. |
